etymology.description: 早(早) (zǎo, "early") indicates the early part of the day, while 上(上) (shàng, "above; upper; on top") suggests the beginning or upper/first segment of the day. Together they indicate the 'morning', the early part that comes before the main part of the day.
etymology.interpretation: 早(早) (zǎo, "early") signifies the time when activities typically begin. As the sun rises from the horizon, it marks the transition from night to day. 上(上) (shàng, "above; upper; on top") conveys the upper half of the day after night, delineating the morning as the start or upper section of the daytime sequence.

examples.chinese: 我每天早上(我每天早上)都去跑步(都去跑步)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshang dōu qù pǎobù.

examples.chinese: 早上(早上)的空气(的空氣)很清新(很清新)。
examples.english: The morning air is very fresh.

examples.chinese: 我们计划在早上(我們計劃在早上)出发(出發)。
examples.english: We plan to depart in the morning.
